generatedSentence: The forgotten `tideway` to an `aurora` once fueled `filmmaking` `energies` in our `galaxy`. An `inhuman` `whistleblower` `knuckled` down, remembering `kisses` from `lifeforms` `long` past. His `husky` `panther` companion, `downwind` of danger, guarded the `dashboard` of their `caravel`. He carried `onyx` `grinders` for `cacao`, a `kind` act for his `niece` whose `pony` plushie, adorned with `feathers`, offered a `tick` of `uncomfortable` `lifestyle` but hope for the `generation`. Unfortunately, `withdrawals` of precious `iridium` `liquids` became `difficulty`, requiring a `ransom` from a `brainwashed` `marshal`. An `oink` `gargantuan` `tank` `unleashed` `ripples` of `tension`, making `festivities` feel `bleak`. His `backstory` hinted at `nine` `humors` and `merchandising` a `volatile` `viscosity` that made `control` `clumsy`. He knew he must `remember` the `past`, a `rocky` `relaunch` of `kayaks` through `sunflowers` and `hawthorn` near his `country` home, was his `warranty` for `understanding`. The `epic` `anthology` of this new `generation` `occurred` `frantic`ly, a `minute` after the `evacuation` call. He `injects` himself with a `randomize`d serum, his `jaws` set `upright` against the `gunpowder` smell. The `examination` of the `exterior` showed `enzymes` interacting with `acrylic` in an `unbelievable` way. He `narrowly` escaped the `dejection` of the `mazurka` of chaos, leaving `pajamas` and `fireflies` behind, a `factual` `imagery` of `ambiguous` `instants` in `outer space`. He felt `himself` `ejects` from the `caravel`, a final `knock` as he chose to `retry` his `happiest` `holidays` memory. Using the `fertilizer` of experience, he knew his `hymnody` for peace was `normal` now. The `vagrant` existence had ended. He was `happy`, having finally escaped the `tick` of `inhuman`ity, embracing his `kind` nature and the promise of a brighter `generation`.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A protagonist with short cropped grey hair and cybernetic arm, wearing a patched-up space jumpsuit. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist pilots a sleek, worn-down spaceship, looking out at a swirling aurora and distant galaxy, full-body shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist stands before a massive, menacing futuristic tank, an intimidating marshal figure in the foreground, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, in a frantic action pose, injects himself with a glowing serum, debris flying around a futuristic, ruined cityscape, dynamic high-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist floats in open space, smiling serenely as distant stars twinkle, a small, peaceful planet visible below, wide shot.

definitions.definition: A channel in which the tide sets.

An atmospheric phenomenon created by charged particles from the sun striking the upper atmosphere, creating coloured lights in the sky. It is usually named australis or borealis based on whether it is in the Southern or Northern Hemisphere respectively.
The activity of preparing edited video works, formerly principally films, whether for entertainment or other purposes.
In a narrow manner; without flexibility or latitude.
definitions.example: They regarded the new idea rather narrowly.

definitions.definition: By a narrow margin; closely.
definitions.example: They narrowly escaped collision.

definitions.definition: The impetus behind all motion and all activity.
The capacity to do work.
A quantity that denotes the ability to do work and is measured in a unit dimensioned in mass × distance²/time² (ML²/T²) or the equivalent.

An intangible, modifiable force (often characterized as either 'positive' or 'negative') believed in some New Age religions to emanate from a person, place or thing and which is (or can be) preserved and transferred in human interactions; shared mood or group habit; a vibe, a feeling, an impression. (Compare aura.)

(often plural) The external actions and influences resulting from an entity’s internal nature (ousia) and by which it is made manifest, as opposed to that internal nature itself; the aspect of an entity that can affect the wider world and be apprehended by other beings.
A measure of how many actions a player or unit can take; in the fantasy genre often called magic points or mana.
Of or pertaining to inhumanity and the indifferently cruel, sadistic or barbaric behavior it brings.
The writing, composing, or singing of hymns or psalms.
The hymns of a particular church or of a particular time.
A high-ranking officer in the household of a medieval prince or lord, who was originally in charge of the cavalry and later the military forces in general.
A military officer of the highest rank in several countries, including France and the former Soviet Union; equivalent to a general of the army in the United States. See also field marshal.
A person in charge of the ceremonial arrangement and management of a gathering.
A federal lawman.
To arrange (troops, etc.) in line for inspection or a parade.
(by extension) To arrange (facts, etc.) in some methodical order.
To ceremoniously guide, conduct or usher.
To gather data for transmission.
Receiving from someone's care what one has earlier entrusted to them. Usually refers to money.
A method of birth control which consists of removing the penis from the vagina before ejaculation.
A type of metabolic shock the body undergoes when a substance, usually a toxin such as heroin, to which a patient is dependent is withheld. Sometimes used with the substance as modifier.
definitions.example: caffeine withdrawal

definitions.definition: An act of withdrawing.
The sum of money taken from a bank account.
One who reports a problem or violation to the authorities; especially, an employee or former employee who reports a violation by an employer.
definitions.example: We owe it to a small group of brave whistle-blowers that we know about the infractions at all.

definitions.definition: To apply pressure, or rub or massage with one's knuckles.
definitions.example: He knuckled the sleep from his eyes.

definitions.definition: To bend the fingers.
To touch one's forehead as a mark of respect.
To yield.
(chiefly in combination) Having a certain type of knuckles.
To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.
To (cause to) touch lightly or slightly; to come into contact.
definitions.example: His ball kissed the black into the corner pocket.

definitions.definition: Of two or more people, to touch each other's lips together, usually to express love or affection or passion.
To treat with fondness.
A touch with the lips, usually to express love or affection, or as a greeting.
An 'X' mark placed at the end of a letter or other type of message.
A type of filled chocolate candy, shaped as if someone had kissed the top. See Hershey's Kisses.
Any specific living organism.
A tiny woodland arachnid of the suborder Ixodida.
A banded variety of chalcedony, a cryptocrystalline form of quartz.
A jet-black color, named after the gemstone.
Any of various lycaenid butterflies of the genus Horaga.
Jet-black
In the same direction as the wind is blowing
(+ from) positioned relative to something in such a way that it can be smelled in the wind
In the direction opposite that of landing in a traffic pattern
A panel under the windscreen of a motor car or aircraft, containing indicator dials, compartments, and sometimes controls.

An upturned screen of wood or leather placed on the front of a horse-drawn carriage, sleigh or other vehicle that protected the driver from mud, debris, water and snow thrown up by the horse's hooves.
A graphical user interface in the form of or resembling a motor car dashboard.
definitions.example: Log in to the dashboard to change your account settings.

definitions.definition: The float of a paddle wheel.
To organize in a dashboard format.
definitions.example: Dashboarding your work can enhance productivity.

definitions.definition: Any of various shrubs and small trees of the genus Crataegus having small, apple-like fruits and thorny branches
(of a voice) Hoarse and rough-sounding.
Burly, stout.
Abounding with husks; consisting of husks.
Any of various big cats with black fur; most especially, the black-coated leopard of India.
Any big cat of the genus Panthera.
A cougar; especially the Florida panther.
A creature resembling a big cat with a multicolored hide, found in Ancient Greek mythology.
A person who wanders from place to place; a nomad, a wanderer.
(specifically) A person without settled employment or habitation who supports himself or herself by begging or some dishonest means; a tramp, a vagabond.
definitions.example: Every morning before work, I see that poor vagrant around the neighbourhood begging for food.

definitions.definition: Vagrans egista, a widely distributed Asian butterfly of the family Nymphalidae.
An animal, typically a bird, found outside its species' usual range.
Any plant of the genus Helianthus, so called probably from the form and color of its floral head, having the form of a large disk surrounded by yellow ray flowers.
A bright yellow, like that of the flower petals.
Any flat, radially symmetric organic compound such as coronene
A guarantee that a certain outcome or obligation will be fulfilled; security.

An obsolete legal agreement that was a real covenant and ran with the land, whereby the grantor and his heirs of a piece of real estate held in freehold were required to officially guarantee their claim and plead one’s case for the title. If evicted by someone with a superior claim (paramount title) they were also required to hand over other real estate of equal value in recompense. It has now been replaced by personal covenants and the covenant of warranty.

A legal agreement, either written or oral (an expressed warranty) or implied through the actions of the buyer and seller (an implied warranty), which states that the goods or property in question will be in exactly the same state as promised, such as in a sale of an item or piece of real estate.
A written guarantee, usually over a fixed period, provided to someone who buys a product or item, which states that repairs will be provided free of charge in case of damage or a fault.
definitions.example: I made sure to check the terms of my warranty for my computer to ensure I was covered in case it broke down.

definitions.definition: (insurance law) A stipulation of an insurance policy made by an insuree, guaranteeing that the facts of the policy are true and the insurance risk is as stated, which if not fulfilled renders the policy void.
Justification or mandate to do something, especially in terms of one’s personal conduct.
To warrant; to guarantee.
A style of living that reflects the attitudes and values of a person or group.

The totality of the likes and dislikes of a particular section of the market, especially when expressed in terms of the products and services that they would buy; a marketing strategy based on the self-image of such a group.

A substance that is flowing, and keeping no shape, such as water; a substance of which the molecules, while not tending to separate from one another like those of a gas, readily change their relative position, and which therefore retains no definite shape, except that determined by the containing receptacle; an inelastic fluid.
definitions.example: A liquid can freeze to become a solid or evaporate into a gas.

definitions.definition: A class of consonant sounds that includes l and r.
Money paid for the freeing of a hostage.
definitions.example: They were held for two million dollars ransom.

definitions.definition: The release of a captive, or of captured property, by payment of a consideration.
definitions.example: prisoners hopeless of ransom

definitions.definition: A sum paid for the pardon of some great offence and the discharge of the offender; also, a fine paid in lieu of corporal punishment.
(14th century) To deliver, especially in context of sin or relevant penalties.
To pay a price to set someone free from captivity or punishment.
definitions.example: to ransom prisoners from an enemy

definitions.definition: To exact a ransom for, or a payment on.
definitions.example: Such lands as he had rule of he ransomed them so grievously, and would tax the men two or three times in a year. — Berners.

definitions.definition: The sound made by a pig, or an imitation thereof.
definitions.example: The protesters replied to the police officers' demands with a chorus of oinks.

definitions.definition: Of a pig or in imitation thereof, to make its characteristic sound.
definitions.example: The hogs oinked happily in their pen as the farmer poured slop in their feeding trough.

definitions.definition: Representing the sound made by a pig.
Drawing attention to male chauvinism (from the term male chauvinist pig).
Any beetle of the family Lampyridae, which exhibit bioluminescence during twilight.
The state of being difficult, or hard to do.
An obstacle that hinders achievement of a goal.
definitions.example: We faced a difficulty in trying to book a flight so late.

definitions.definition: (sometimes in the plural) Physical danger from the environment, especially with risk of drowning
definitions.example: 2012 August 2, "Children rescued after getting into difficulties in Donegal" BBC Online

definitions.definition: An objection.
That which cannot be easily understood or believed.
An awkward situation or quarrel.
The digit or figure 9.
A playing card with nine pips.
A nine-millimeter semi-automatic pistol.
(usually in the plural) A statistical unit of proportion (of reliability, purity, etc.).
definitions.example: They guaranteed that our Web site would have 99.99% uptime, or four nines.

definitions.definition: A baseball club, team, or lineup (composed of nine players).
A numerical value equal to 9; the number following eight and preceding ten.
Describing a group or set with nine elements.
definitions.example: A cat has nine lives.

definitions.definition: The quality of being amusing, comical, funny.
definitions.example: She has a great sense of humour, and I always laugh a lot whenever we get together.

definitions.definition: A mood, especially a bad mood; a temporary state of mind or disposition brought upon by an event; an abrupt illogical inclination or whim.
definitions.example: He was in a particularly vile humour that afternoon.

definitions.definition: Any of the fluids in an animal body, especially the four "cardinal humours" of blood, yellow bile, black bile and phlegm that were believed to control the health and mood of the human body.
Either of the two regions of liquid within the eyeball, the aqueous humour and vitreous humour.
Moist vapour, moisture.
To pacify by indulging.
definitions.example: I know you don't believe my story, but humour me for a minute and imagine it to be true.

definitions.definition: To grasp a concept fully and thoroughly, especially (of words, statements, art, etc.) to be aware of the meaning of and (of people) to be aware of the intent of.
definitions.example: I'm sorry. I don't understand.

definitions.definition: To believe, to think one grasps sufficiently despite potentially incomplete knowledge.
definitions.example: I understand that you have a package for me?

definitions.definition: (obsolete outside circus, acrobatics) To stand underneath, to support.

synonyms: pick up what someone is putting down

definitions.definition: (gerund) The act of one that understands or comprehends; comprehension; knowledge; discernment.
Reason or intelligence, ability to grasp the full meaning of knowledge, ability to infer.
Opinion, judgement or outlook.
definitions.example: According to my understanding, the situation is quite perilous. I wonder if you see it this way, too.

definitions.definition: An agreement of minds; harmony; something mutually understood or agreed upon.
Sympathy.
definitions.example: He showed much understanding for my problems when he heard about my past.

definitions.definition: Showing compassion.
Knowing; skilful.
Not comfortable; causing discomfort.
definitions.example: The class squirmed and fidgeted in the uncomfortable new chairs.

definitions.definition: Experiencing discomfort.
Uneasy or anxious.
definitions.example: Sharing a house with them made me uncomfortable.

definitions.definition: Put off or disgusted.
Meat from the shoulder of a cow or other animal.
Food.
A mechanical device that holds an object firmly in place, for example holding a drill bit in a high-speed rotating drill or grinder.
To place in a chuck, or hold by means of a chuck, as in turning.
To bore or turn (a hole) in a revolving piece held in a chuck.
A chicken, a hen.
A clucking sound.
A friend or close acquaintance; term of endearment.
definitions.example: Are you all right, chuck?

definitions.definition: To make a clucking sound.
To call, as a hen her chickens.
A gentle touch or tap.
definitions.example: She gave him an affectionate chuck under the chin.

definitions.definition: A casual throw.
A throw, an incorrect bowling action.
An act or instance of vomiting.
On rhythm guitar or mandolin etc., the muting of a chord by lifting the fretting fingers immediately after strumming, producing a percussive effect.
To touch or tap gently.
To throw, especially in a careless or inaccurate manner.
definitions.example: Chuck that magazine to me, would you?

definitions.definition: To throw; to bowl with an incorrect action.
To discard, to throw away.
definitions.example: This food's gone off - you'd better chuck it.

definitions.definition: To jilt; to dump.
definitions.example: She's chucked me for another man!

definitions.definition: To vomit.
To leave; to depart; to bounce.
definitions.example: Let's chuck.

definitions.definition: To chuckle; to laugh.
On rhythm guitar or mandolin etc.: to mute a chord by lifting the fretting fingers immediately after strumming, producing a percussive effect.
A small pebble.
(in the plural) Money.
A rodent of the family Sciuridae, belonging to the group of large ground squirrels known as marmots, Marmota monax.
To engage in trade; to carry on commerce.
To engage in in-store promotion of the sale of goods, as by display and arrangement of goods.
definitions.example: He started his career merchandising in a small clothing store chain.

definitions.definition: To engage in the trade of.
To engage in in-store promotion of the sale of.
definitions.example: He got hired to merchandise some new sporting goods lines.

definitions.definition: To promote as if for sale.
definitions.example: The record companies don't get as good a return on merchandising artists under contract.

definitions.definition: Trade in merchandise
A Polish folk dance in triple time, usually moderately fast, containing a heavy accent on the third beat and occasionally the second beat.
A classical musical composition inspired by the folk dance and conforming in some respects to its form, particularly as popularized by Chopin.
A figure skating move that combines elements of the toe loop and bunny-hop.
A chemical or compound that changes into a gas easily.
Evaporating or vaporizing readily under normal conditions.
(of a substance) Explosive.
(of a price etc) Variable or erratic.
(of a person) Quick to become angry or violent.
Fickle.
Temporary or ephemeral.
(of a situation) Potentially violent.
(of a variable) Having its associated memory immediately updated with any changes in value.
(of memory) Whose content is lost when the computer is powered down
Passing through the air on wings, or by the buoyant force of the atmosphere; flying; having the power to fly.
The state of being viscous.
A quantity expressing the magnitude of internal friction in a fluid, as measured by the force per unit area resisting uniform flow.
A tendency to prolong interpersonal encounters.
The previous life and experiences of a person, especially a character in a dramatic work, but also a real-life person.
A fictitious account of a person's history designed to conceal their actual history and motives.
A prequel.
The condition of being held in a state between two or more forces, which are acting in opposition to each other.
Psychological state of being tense.
A feeling of nervousness, excitement, or fear that is created in a movie, book, etc.; suspense.
State of an elastic object which is stretched in a way which increases its length.

Force transmitted through a rope, string, cable, or similar object (used with prepositions on, in, or of, e.g., "The tension in the cable is 1000 N", to convey that the same magnitude of force applies to objects attached to both ends).

Voltage. Usually only the terms low tension, high tension, and extra-high tension, and the abbreviations LT, HT, and EHT are used. They are not precisely defined; LT is normally a few volts, HT a few hundreds of volts, and EHT thousands of volts.
To place an object in tension, to pull or place strain on.
definitions.example: We tensioned the cable until it snapped.

definitions.definition: Unstable; easily rocked.
definitions.example: The table was rocky, so we put a book under one leg.

definitions.definition: In the style of rock music.
definitions.example: His new album is quite rocky.

definitions.definition: Troubled; or difficult; in danger or distress.
definitions.example: Their relationship had weathered some rocky times, but they loved each other.

definitions.definition: A subsequent launch.
To launch again.
A moving disturbance, or undulation, in the surface of a fluid.
definitions.example: I dropped a small stone into the pond and watched the ripples spread.

definitions.definition: A sound similar to that of undulating water.
A style of ice cream in which flavors have been coarsely blended together.
definitions.example: I enjoy fudge ripple ice cream, but I especially like to dig through the carton to get at the ripple part and eat only that.

definitions.definition: A small oscillation of an otherwise steady signal.
To move like the undulating surface of a body of water; to undulate.
To propagate like a moving wave.
To make a sound as of water running gently over a rough bottom, or the breaking of ripples on the shore.
To shape into a series of ripples.
To launch or unleash in rapid succession.
To scratch, tear, or break slightly; graze
An implement, with teeth like those of a comb, for removing the seeds and seed vessels from flax, broom corn, etc.
To remove the seeds from (the stalks of flax, etc.), by means of a ripple.
(often pluralized) A festival or similar celebration.
An experience or expression of celebratory feeling, merriment, gaiety.
A state of melancholy or depression; low spirits, the blues.
The act of humbling or abasing oneself.
A low condition; weakness; inability.
Defecation or feces.
Clothes for wearing to bed and sleeping in, usually consisting of a loose-fitting shirt and pants/trousers.
Loose-fitting trousers worn by both sexes in various southern Asian countries including India.
An explosive mixture of saltpetre (potassium nitrate), charcoal and sulphur; formerly used in gunnery but now mostly used in fireworks.
Short for gunpowder tea.
One who grinds something, such as the teeth.
(anatomical) A molar.
A power tool with a spinning abrasive disc, used for grinding, smoothing, and shaping materials, usually metal.
A sandwich made on a long, cylindrical roll.
definitions.example: I am going to the deli to get a grinder for lunch.

definitions.definition: A kitchen gadget for processing coffee, herbs etc. into small or powdered pieces
The restless flycatcher (Myiagra inquieta) of Australia, which makes a noise like a scissors grinder.
A fan or performer of grindcore music.
A biohacker who uses cybernetic implants or biochemicals to enhance or change their own body.
A student who studies hard; a swot.
A person who coaches students for an upcoming examination.
(ice hockey slang) A hard-working, physical player with limited offensive ability.
A low-ranking attorney with no clients who works very hard.
An outdoor space for drills and parades.
The Milky Way; the apparent band of concentrated stars which appears in the night sky over earth.
Any of the collections of many millions or billions of stars, galactic dust, black holes, etc. existing as independent and coherent systems, of which there are billions in the known universe.
Any print or pattern reminiscent of a galaxy, generally consisting of blending, semiopaque patches of vibrant color on a dark background.
An acrylic resin.
A paint containing an acrylic resin.
definitions.example: When it comes to painting, I prefer using acrylics as my medium.

definitions.definition: A painting executed using such a paint.
A thick sheet of plastic.
A liquid coating system based on an acrylic resin
Derived from acrylic acid or acrylonitrile.
Containing an acrylic resin.
To recall from one's memory; to have an image in one's memory.
To memorize; to put something into memory.
definitions.example: Please remember this formula!

definitions.definition: To keep in mind, be mindful of
definitions.example: Remember what I've said.

definitions.definition: To not forget (to do something required)
definitions.example: Remember to lock the door when you go out.

definitions.definition: To convey greetings from.
definitions.example: Please remember me to your brother.

definitions.definition: To put in mind; to remind (also used reflexively)
To engage in the process of recalling memories.
definitions.example: You don't have to remind him; he remembers very well.

definitions.definition: To give (a person) money as a token of appreciation of past service or friendship.
definitions.example: My aunt remembered me in her will, leaving me several thousand pounds.

definitions.definition: To commemorate, to have a remembrance ceremony
To arrange randomly; to make random
definitions.example: He randomised the marbles by spilling them onto the floor.

definitions.definition: A natural substance that is used to make the ground more suitable for growing plants.
A chemical compound created to have the same effect.
The act of evacuating; leaving a place in an orderly fashion, especially for safety.
Withdrawal of troops or civils from a town, fortress, etc.
The act of emptying, clearing of the contents, or discharging, including creating a vacuum.
Voidance of any matter by the natural passages of the body or by an artificial opening; defecation; also, a diminution of the fluids of an animal body by cathartics, venesection, or other means.
That which is evacuated or discharged; especially, a discharge by stool or other natural means.
Abolition; nullification.
The act of examining.
Particularly, an inspection by a medical professional to establish the extent and nature of any sickness or injury.
A formal test involving answering written or oral questions under a time constraint and usually without access to textbooks.
Interrogation.
A chemical element (symbol Ir) with an atomic number of 77: a very hard, brittle, silvery-white transition metal of the platinum group.
A single atom of this element.
A very short period of time; a moment.
definitions.example: She paused for only an instant, which was just enough time for John to change the subject.

definitions.definition: A single, usually precise, point in time.
definitions.example: The instant the alarm went off, he fled the building.

definitions.definition: A beverage or food which has been pre-processed to reduce preparation time, especially instant coffee.
A camera that uses self-developing film to create a chemically developed print shortly after the photograph is taken.
A type, race or category; a group of entities that have common characteristics such that they may be grouped together.
definitions.example: This is a strange kind of tobacco.

definitions.definition: A makeshift or otherwise atypical specimen.
definitions.example: The opening served as a kind of window.

definitions.definition: One's inherent nature; character, natural disposition.
Family, lineage.
Manner.
Goods or services used as payment, as e.g. in barter.
Equivalent means used as response to an action.
definitions.example: I'll pay in kind for his insult.

definitions.definition: Each of the two elements of the communion service, bread and wine.
One of the bones, usually bearing teeth, which form the framework of the mouth.
The part of the face below the mouth.
definitions.example: His jaw dropped in amazement.

definitions.definition: Anything resembling the jaw of an animal in form or action; especially plural, the mouth or way of entrance.
definitions.example: the jaws of a pass; the jaws of darkness; the jaws of death.

definitions.definition: A notch or opening.
A notched or forked part, adapted for holding an object in place.
definitions.example: the jaw of a railway-car pedestal.

definitions.definition: One of a pair of opposing parts which are movable towards or from each other, for grasping or crushing anything between them.
definitions.example: the jaws of a vise; the jaws of a stone-crushing machine.

definitions.definition: The inner end of a boom or gaff, hollowed in a half circle so as to move freely on a mast.
Impudent or abusive talk.
Axle guard.
The curved part of the cushion marking the entry to the pocket.
To assail or abuse by scolding.
To scold; to clamor.
To talk; to converse.
(of a ball) To stick in the jaws of a pocket.
The mouth
The borders of anything which has a mouthlike aspect.
definitions.example: Open the jaws of the wrench and wide as possible.

definitions.definition: (eastern Ohio) a contrarian.
The fact of creating something, or bringing something into being; production, creation.
The act of creating a living creature or organism; procreation.
Race, family; breed.
A single step or stage in the succession of natural descent; a rank or degree in genealogy, the members of a family from the same parents, considered as a single unit.
definitions.example: This is the book of the generations of Adam - Genesis 5:1

definitions.definition: Descendants, progeny; offspring.
The average amount of time needed for children to grow up and have children of their own, generally considered to be a period of around thirty years, used as a measure of time.
A set stage in the development of computing or of a specific technology.

The formation or production of any geometrical magnitude, as a line, a surface, a solid, by the motion, in accordance with a mathematical law, of a point or a magnitude, by the motion of a point, of a surface by a line, a sphere by a semicircle, etc.
definitions.example: the generation of a line or curve

definitions.definition: A specific age range whose members can relate culturally to one another.
definitions.example: Generation X grew up in the eighties, whereas the generation known as the millennials grew up in the nineties.

definitions.definition: A version of a form of pop culture which differs from later or earlier versions.
definitions.example: People sometimes dispute which generation of Star Trek is best, including the original and The Next Generation.

definitions.definition: A copy of a recording made from an earlier copy and thus further degraded in quality.
A nocturnal omnivore native to North America, typically with a mixture of gray, brown, and black fur, a mask-like marking around the eyes and a striped tail; Procyon lotor.
Any mammal of the genus Procyon.
Any mammal of the subfamily Procyoninae, a procyonine.
Any mammal of the family Procyonidae, a procyonid.
To happen or take place.
definitions.example: The liftoff will occur in exactly twelve seconds.

definitions.definition: To present or offer itself.
definitions.example: I will write if the opportunity occurs.

definitions.definition: To come or be presented to the mind; to suggest itself.
To be present or found.
definitions.example: The chemical monofluoroacetate occurs in all parts of Dichapetalum cymosum, and is responsible for its toxic effects.

definitions.definition: Happening through bad luck, or because of some unfortunate event.
Used (as a parenthetical word) to express disappointment, compassion, sorrow, regret or grief.
(chiefly British) An area of land; a district, region.

A set region of land having particular human occupation or agreed limits, especially inhabited by members of the same race, speakers of the same language etc., or associated with a given person, occupation, species etc.
The territory of a nation, especially an independent nation state or formerly independent nation; a political entity asserting ultimate authority over a geographical area; a sovereign state.
(usually preceded by “the”) A rural area, as opposed to a town or city; the countryside.
The rock through which a vein runs.
The female genitalia, especially the vagina.
From or in the countryside or connected with it.
Of or connected to country music.
A style of popular music that originated in the folk music of the rural population of the southern and western United States and characterized by string-band instruments and simple melodies.
Sudden fatigue as a result of glycogen depletion from not having taken in enough nutrition.
An abrupt rapping sound, as from an impact of a hard object against wood.

definitions.definition: A sharp impact.
definitions.example: He took a knock on the head.

definitions.definition: Criticism.
Preignition, a type of abnormal combustion occurring in spark ignition engines caused by self-ignition; also, the characteristic knocking sound associated with it.
A batsman's innings.
definitions.example: He played a slow but sure knock of 35.

definitions.definition: To strike for admittance; to rap upon, as a door.
To criticize verbally; to denigrate; to undervalue.
definitions.example: Don’t knock it until you’ve tried it.

definitions.definition: To kick a ball towards another player; to pass.
To impress forcibly or strongly; to astonish; to move to admiration or applause.
To bump or impact.
definitions.example: I accidentally knocked my drink off the bar.

definitions.definition: To rap one's knuckles against something, especially wood.
definitions.example: Knock on the door and find out if they’re home.

definitions.definition: Another attempt.
To try or attempt again.
definitions.example: to retry connecting to the Internet after an error

definitions.definition: To try judicially a second time.
definitions.example: to retry a case

definitions.definition: A day on which a festival, religious event, or national celebration is traditionally observed.
definitions.example: Today is a Wiccan holiday!

definitions.definition: A day declared free from work by the state or government.
A period of one or more days taken off work for leisure and often travel; often plural.
A period during which pupils do not attend their school; often plural; rarely used for students at university (usually: vacation).
definitions.example: I want to take a French course this summer holiday.

definitions.definition: A period during which, by agreement, the usual payments are not made.
definitions.example: a mortgage payment holiday

definitions.definition: A gap in coverage, e.g. of paint on a surface, or sonar imagery.
To take a period of time away from work or study.
To spend a period of time for travel.
(usually after possessive) holiday, vacation; time off work, especially if spent travelling away from home
The season around Christmas, Hanukkah, and New Year's Day.
definitions.example: Where will you be spending the holidays?

definitions.definition: To free from a leash, or as from a leash.
definitions.example: He unleashed his dog in the park.

definitions.definition: To let go; to release.
definitions.example: He unleashed his fury.

definitions.definition: To precipitate; to bring about.
Not leashed; without a leash on it.
Freed from any restraint, physical or otherwise.
definitions.example: unleashed potential

definitions.definition: Of the giant Gargantua or his appetite.
Huge; immense; tremendous.
A closed container for liquids or gases.
An open container or pool for storing water or other liquids.
A pond, pool, or small lake, natural or artificial.
The fuel reservoir of a vehicle.
The amount held by a container; a tankful.
definitions.example: I burned three tanks of gas on the drive to New York.

definitions.definition: An armoured fighting vehicle, armed with a gun, and moving on caterpillar tracks.
(Australian and Indian English) A reservoir or dam.
A large metal container for holding drinking water for animals, usually placed near a wind-driven water pump, in an animal pen or field.
By extension a small pond for the same purpose.
A very muscular and physically imposing person. Somebody who is built like a tank.
A unit or character designed primarily around damage absorption and holding the attention of the enemy (as opposed to dealing damage, healing, or other tasks).
A prison cell, or prison generally.
A metaphorical place where a player goes to contemplate a decision; see in the tank.
To fail or fall (often used in describing the economy or the stock market); to degenerate or decline rapidly; to plummet.
To attract the attacks of an enemy target in cooperative team-based combat, so that one's teammates can defeat the enemy in question more efficiently.
To put (fuel, etc.) into a tank.
To deliberately lose a sports match with the intent of gaining a perceived future competitive advantage.
To resist damage; to be attacked without being hurt.
To contemplate a decision for a long time; to go in the tank.
A person who is insane or mentally unstable, madman.
Insane, mentally unstable.
In a state of panic, worry, frenzy or rush.
definitions.example: They returned the missing child to his frantic mother.

definitions.definition: Extremely energetic
definitions.example: frantic music

definitions.definition: To push or pump (something, especially fluids) into a cavity or passage.
definitions.example: The nurse injected a painkilling drug into the veins of my forearm.

definitions.definition: To introduce (something) suddenly or violently.
definitions.example: Punk injected a much-needed sense of urgency into the British music scene.

definitions.definition: To administer an injection to (someone or something), especially of medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: Now lie back while we inject you with the anesthetic.

definitions.definition: To take or be administered something by means of injection, especially medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: It's been a week since I stopped injecting, and I'm still in withdrawal.

definitions.definition: To introduce (code) into an existing program or its memory space, often without tight integration and sometimes through a security vulnerability.
To cast or throw; used with on.
A light, usually lateen-rigged sailing ship used by the Portuguese and Spanish for about 300 years from the 15th century, first for trade and later for voyages of exploration.
To affect one's mind by using extreme mental pressure or any other mind-affecting process. (e.g. hypnosis)
To take from an electronically controlled machine its stored-up information; to erase a computer's programming. (1960)
A collection of literary works, such as poems or short stories, especially a collection from various authors.
Of a work or series containing various stories with no direct relation to one another.
(by extension) An assortment of things.
The study of flowers.
An extended narrative poem in elevated or dignified language, celebrating the feats of a deity, demigod (heroic epic), other legend or traditional hero.
definitions.example: The Icelandic epic took all night to recite.

definitions.definition: A series of events considered appropriate to an epic.
definitions.example: The book was an epic in four volumes.

definitions.definition: In software development, a large or extended user story.
Of, or relating to, an epic.
definitions.example: Beowulf is an epic poem.

definitions.definition: Momentously heroic; grand in scale or character
definitions.example: The epic defense was rewarded with the highest military decorations

definitions.definition: Extending beyond the usual or ordinary.
definitions.example: The after-prom party was truly epic.

definitions.definition: Any vertical part of a structure, especially one of the goal posts in sports.
A word clued by the successive initial, middle, or final letters of the cross-lights in a double acrostic or triple acrostic.
An upright piano.
Short for upright vacuum cleaner.
To set upright or stand back up (something that has fallen).
Vertical; erect.
definitions.example: I was standing upright, waiting for my orders.

definitions.definition: Greater in height than breadth.
Of good morals; practicing ethical values.
(of a golf club) Having the head approximately at a right angle with the shaft.
In or into an upright position
Not to be believed.
Incredible; so surprising it is almost unable to believe.
definitions.example: The most unbelievable thing happened to me today!

definitions.definition: Implausible or improbable.
definitions.example: His excuse seems rather unbelievable.

definitions.definition: A tree, Theobroma cacao, whose seed is used to make chocolate.
This tree's seed, the cocoa bean.
A branching, hair-like structure that grows on the bodies of birds, used for flight, swimming, protection and display.
Long hair on the lower legs of a dog or horse, especially a draft horse, notably the Clydesdale breed. Narrowly only the rear hair.
One of the fins or wings on the shaft of an arrow.
A longitudinal strip projecting from an object to strengthen it, or to enter a channel in another object and thereby prevent displacement sideways but permit motion lengthwise; a spline.
Kind; nature; species (from the proverbial phrase "birds of a feather").

One of the two shims of the three-piece stone-splitting tool known as plug and feather or plug and feathers; the feathers are placed in a borehole and then a wedge is driven between them, causing the stone to split.
The angular adjustment of an oar or paddle-wheel float, with reference to a horizontal axis, as it leaves or enters the water.
Anything petty or trifling; a whit or jot.
(in the plural) Partridges and pheasants, as opposed to rabbits and hares (called fur).
To cover or furnish with feathers.
To arrange in the manner or appearance of feathers.
definitions.example: The stylist feathered my hair.

definitions.definition: To rotate the oars while they are out of the water to reduce wind resistance.
To streamline the blades of an aircraft's propeller by rotating them perpendicular to the axis of the propeller when the engine is shut down so that the propeller does not windmill during flight.
definitions.example: After striking the bird, the pilot feathered the left, damaged engine’s propeller.

definitions.definition: To finely shave or bevel an edge.
To intergrade or blend the pixels of an image with those of a background or neighboring image.
To adorn, as with feathers; to fringe.
To render light as a feather; to give wings to.
To enrich; to exalt; to benefit.
To tread, as a cock.
To accidentally touch the cue ball with the tip of the cue when taking aim
A line or vector that is perpendicular to another line, surface, or plane.
A person who is normal, who fits into mainstream society, as opposed to those who live alternative lifestyles.
The usual state.
definitions.example: Heavy workload is the new normal.

definitions.definition: According to norms or rules or to a regular pattern.
definitions.example: Organize the data into third normal form.

definitions.definition: Usual, healthy; not sick or ill or unlike oneself.
definitions.example: John is feeling normal again.

definitions.definition: (of a school) teaching teachers how to teach (to certain norms)
definitions.example: My grandmother attended Mankato State Normal School.

definitions.definition: Of, relating to, or being a solution containing one equivalent weight of solute per litre of solution.
Describing a straight chain isomer of an aliphatic hydrocarbon, or an aliphatic compound in which a substituent is in the 1- position of such a hydrocarbon.
(of a mode in an oscillating system) In which all parts of an object vibrate at the same frequency (see normal mode).
(of points) In the default position, set for the most frequently used route.
Perpendicular to a tangent of a curve or derivative of a surface.
definitions.example: The interior normal vector of an ideal perfect sphere will always point toward the center, and the exterior normal vector directly away, and both will always be co-linear with the ray whose' tip ends at the point of intersection, which is the intersection of all three sets of points.

definitions.definition: The outside part, parts or surface of something.
definitions.example: The sticker was attached to the exterior of the package

definitions.definition: Foreign lands.
definitions.example: She is our new minister of the exterior

definitions.definition: Relating to the outside parts or surface of something.
definitions.example: the exterior part of a sphere

definitions.definition: Being from outside a country; foreign.
definitions.example: the exterior relations of a state or kingdom

definitions.definition: Outdoor.
A globular protein that catalyses a biological chemical reaction.
Leavened bread, as opposed to azyme
A unit of time equal to sixty seconds (one-sixtieth of an hour).
definitions.example: You have twenty minutes to complete the test.

definitions.definition: A short but unspecified time period.
definitions.example: Wait a minute, I’m not ready yet!

definitions.definition: A unit of angle equal to one-sixtieth of a degree.
definitions.example: We need to be sure these maps are accurate to within one minute of arc.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural, minutes) A (usually formal) written record of a meeting or a part of a meeting.
definitions.example: Let’s look at the minutes of last week’s meeting.

definitions.definition: A unit of purchase on a telephone or other network, especially a cell phone network, roughly equivalent in gross form to sixty seconds' use of the network.
definitions.example: If you buy this phone, you’ll get 100 free minutes.

definitions.definition: A point in time; a moment.
A nautical or a geographic mile.
An old coin, a half farthing.
A very small part of anything, or anything very small; a jot; a whit.
A fixed part of a module.
A while or a long unspecified period of time
definitions.example: Oh, I ain't heard that song in a minute!

definitions.definition: Of an event, to write in a memo or the minutes of a meeting.
definitions.example: I’ll minute this evening’s meeting.

definitions.definition: To set down a short sketch or note of; to jot down; to make a minute or a brief summary of.
A long vowel.
A long syllable.
A note formerly used in music, one half the length of a large, twice that of a breve.
A long integer variable, twice the size of an int, two or four times the size of a short, and half of a long long.
An entity with a long position in an asset.
definitions.example: Every uptick made the longs cheer.

definitions.definition: A long-term investment.
The long summer vacation at the English universities.
To take a long position in.
Having much distance from one terminating point on an object or an area to another terminating point (usually applies to horizontal dimensions; see Usage Notes below).
definitions.example: It's a long way from the Earth to the Moon.

definitions.definition: Having great duration.
definitions.example: The pyramids of Egypt have been around for a long time.

definitions.definition: Seemingly lasting a lot of time, because it is boring or tedious or tiring.
Not short; tall.
Possessing or owning stocks, bonds, commodities or other financial instruments with the aim of benefiting of the expected rise in their value.
definitions.example: I'm long in DuPont;  I have a long position in DuPont.

definitions.definition: Of a fielding position, close to the boundary (or closer to the boundary than the equivalent short position).
(of a ball or a shot) Landing beyond the baseline, and therefore deemed to be out.
definitions.example: That forehand is long.

definitions.definition: Occurring or coming after an extended interval; distant in time; far away.
Open to multiple interpretations.
definitions.example: The politician was criticized for his ambiguous statements and lack of precision.

definitions.definition: Vague and unclear.
definitions.example: He gave an ambiguous answer.

definitions.definition: (of persons) Hesitant; uncertain; not taking sides.
Region outside explored space.
Any region of space beyond limits determined with reference to boundaries of a celestial system or body, especially the region of space immediately beyond Earth's atmosphere.
A bluish shade of black.
Pertaining to or consisting of objective claims.
True, accurate, corresponding to reality.
To compel (a person or persons) to leave.
definitions.example: Andrew was ejected from his apartment for not paying the rent.

definitions.definition: To throw out or remove forcefully.
definitions.example: In other news, a Montreal man was ejected from his car when he was involved in an accident.

definitions.definition: To compel (a sports player) to leave the field because of inappropriate behaviour.
To project oneself from an aircraft.
definitions.example: The pilot lost control of the plane and had to eject.

definitions.definition: To cause (something) to come out of a machine.

definitions.definition: To come out of a machine.
definitions.example: I can't get this cassette to  eject.

definitions.definition: An inferred object of someone else's consciousness
(reflexive pronoun) Him; the male object of a verb or preposition that also appears as the subject
definitions.example: He injured himself.

definitions.definition: He; used as an intensifier, often to emphasize that the referent is the exclusive participant in the predicate
definitions.example: He was injured himself.

definitions.definition: The subject or non-reflexive object of a predicate; he himself.
The subject or non-reflexive object of a predicate; he (used of upper-class gentlemen, or sarcastically, of men who imagine themselves to be more important than others)
definitions.example: Has himself come down to breakfast yet?

definitions.definition: A small horse; specifically, any of several small breeds of horse under 14.2 hands at the withers.
A small serving of an alcoholic beverage, especially beer.
definitions.example: 1879, “Some Queer Interviews: Interview with a Pony of Beer”, Puck, Vol. 5–6, p. 435

definitions.definition: (New South Wales, Victoria) A serving of 140 millilitres of beer (formerly 5 fl oz); a quarter pint.
Twenty-five pounds sterling.
A translation used as a study aid; loosely, a crib, a cheat-sheet.
A ponytail hairstyle.
To lead (a horse) from another horse.
To use a crib or cheat-sheet in translating.
To change place.
To change in state or status
To move through time.
To be accepted.
In any game, to decline to play in one's turn.
To do or be better.
To take heed.
The period of time that has already happened, in contrast to the present and the future.
definitions.example: a book about a time machine that can transport people back into the past

definitions.definition: (grammar) The past tense.
Having already happened; in the past; finished.

definitions.definition: (postmodifier) Following expressions of time to indicate how long ago something happened; ago.
Of a period of time: having just gone by; previous.

definitions.definition: (grammar) Of a tense, expressing action that has already happened or a previously-existing state.

definitions.definition: In a direction that passes.
definitions.example: I watched him walk past

definitions.definition: Passing by, especially without stopping or being delayed.
definitions.example: Ignore them, we'll play past them.

definitions.definition: Beyond in place, quantity or time.

definitions.definition: No longer capable of.
definitions.example: I'm past caring what he thinks of me.

definitions.definition: Having recovered or moved on from (a traumatic experience, etc.).
A clumsy person.
Awkward, lacking coordination, not graceful, not dextrous.
definitions.example: He's very clumsy. I wouldn't trust him with carrying the dishes.

definitions.definition: Not elegant or well-planned, lacking tact or subtlety.
definitions.example: It is a clumsy solution, but it might work for now.

definitions.definition: Awkward or inefficient in use or construction, difficult to handle or manage especially because of shape.
Influence or authority over something.
definitions.example: The government has complete control over the situation.

definitions.definition: A separate group or subject in an experiment against which the results are compared where the primary variable is low or non-existent.
The method and means of governing the performance of any apparatus, machine or system, such as a lever, handle or button.
Restraint or ability to contain one's movements or emotions, or self-control.
A security mechanism, policy, or procedure that can counter system attack, reduce risks, and resolve vulnerabilities; a safeguard or countermeasure.
(project management) A means of monitoring for, and triggering intervention in, activities that are not going according to plan.
A duplicate book, register, or account, kept to correct or check another account or register.
An interface element that a computer user interacts with, such as a window or a text box.

Any of the physical factors determining the climate of a place, such as latitude, distribution of land and water, altitude, exposure, prevailing winds, permanent high- or low-barometric-pressure areas, ocean currents, mountain barriers, soil, and vegetation.
A construction in which the understood subject of a given predicate is determined by an expression in context. See control.
To exercise influence over; to suggest or dictate the behavior of.
definitions.example: With a simple remote, he could control the toy truck.

definitions.definition: (construed with for) To design (an experiment) so that the effects of one or more variables are reduced or eliminated.
A type of small boat, covered over by a surface deck, powered by the occupant or occupants using a double-bladed paddle in a sitting position, from a hole in the surface deck
To use a kayak, to travel or race in a kayak.

definitions.definition: To traverse (a body of water) by kayak.
definitions.example: On a dare, he kayaked the Harlem River in New York from Hell's Gate to Spyten Duyvil.

definitions.definition: Without color; pale; pallid.
Desolate and exposed; swept by cold winds.
definitions.example: A bleak and bare rock.

definitions.definition: Unhappy; cheerless; miserable; emotionally desolate.
definitions.example: A bleak future is in store for you.

definitions.definition: A daughter of one’s sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either the daughter of one's brother ("fraternal niece"), or of one's sister ("sororal niece").
definitions.example: My niece just celebrated her 15th birthday.

definitions.definition: The work of one who makes images or visible representation of objects.
Imitation work.
Images in general, or en masse.
Unreal show; imitation; appearance.
The work of the imagination or fancy; false ideas; imaginary phantasms.
Rhetorical decoration in writing or speaking; vivid descriptions presenting or suggesting images of sensible objects; figures in discourse.
A happy event, thing, person, etc.
Preceded by the: happy people as a group.
Often followed by up: to become happy; to brighten up, to cheer up.
Often followed by up: to make happy; to brighten, to cheer, to enliven.
Having a feeling arising from a consciousness of well-being or of enjoyment; enjoying good of any kind, such as comfort, peace, or tranquillity; blissful, contented, joyous.
definitions.example: Music makes me feel happy.

definitions.definition: Experiencing the effect of favourable fortune; favored by fortune or luck; fortunate, lucky, propitious.
Content, satisfied (with or to do something); having no objection (to something).
definitions.example: Are you happy to pay me back by the end of the week?

definitions.definition: (Of acts, speech, etc.) Appropriate, apt, felicitous.

definitions.definition: (in combination) Favoring or inclined to use.

definitions.definition: (of people, often followed by "at" or "in") Dexterous, ready, skilful.
Having a feeling arising from a consciousness of well-being or of enjoyment; enjoying good of any kind, such as comfort, peace, or tranquillity; blissful, contented, joyous.

definitions.definition: Experiencing the effect of favourable fortune; favored by fortune or luck; fortunate, lucky, propitious.
Content, satisfied (with or to do something); having no objection (to something).

definitions.definition: (Of acts, speech, etc.) Appropriate, apt, felicitous.

definitions.definition: (in combination) Favoring or inclined to use.
